schools.summary: The leper's mourning and cry of 'Unclean, unclean': 'and the leper in whom the plague is, his clothes shall be rent, and his head bare, and he shall put a covering upon his upper lip, and shall cry, Unclean, unclean' — the signs of mourning and the cry — 'his clothes shall be rent... and shall cry, Unclean, unclean': the leper to wear the signs of mourning (rent clothes, bare head, covered lip) and to cry 'Unclean, unclean' as a warning (the witnesses noting the significance — the leper's public shame and self-abasement, the rent clothes and bare head, the tokens of mourning and grief, the covered lip, the sign of shame and silence, the cry 'Unclean, unclean,' the public confession and warning to others to keep away, the lesson — the figure of the sinner's estate and confession, the leprosy of sin bringing shame and grief, the sinner mourning his corruption, the cry 'Unclean, unclean' the confession of the penitent who owns his defilement, 'I am a man of unclean lips, and I dwell in the midst of a people of unclean lips,' Isaiah 6:5, 'God be merciful to me a sinner,' Luke 18:13, the publican's confession, the warning to others, the leper warning all to avoid the contagion, the figure of the sinner's warning against the defilement of sin, the figure of the humble, public confession of sin and the shame it brings); the signs of mourning (the rent clothes, bare head, the tokens of grief and abasement) — the figure (the sinner mourning his corruption, the shame of sin, the abasement, the leper humbled); 'shall cry, Unclean, unclean' (the public confession and warning, the leper owning his uncleanness, warning others, the figure, the confession of the penitent, Isaiah 6:5; Luke 18:13, the providence, the leper's shame and confession, the figure of the sinner) — Wesley, Henry, Gill.

schools.summary: The leper dwelling alone without the camp: 'all the days wherein the plague shall be in him he shall be defiled; he is unclean: he shall dwell alone; without the camp shall his habitation be' — the casting out — 'he shall dwell alone; without the camp shall his habitation be': the leper defiled all the days of his plague, dwelling alone, his habitation outside the camp (the witnesses noting the significance — the leper's separation, cast out from the camp, from the fellowship of the people, and from the sanctuary and the presence of God in the midst of the camp, dwelling alone, isolated, excluded, the lesson — the figure of what sin does, sin separating the sinner from God and from his people, casting him out from fellowship, 'your iniquities have separated between you and your God,' Isaiah 59:2, the leprosy of sin excluding from the camp, from the presence of God, the loneliness and exclusion of the sinner, the gravity, the leper dwelling alone, the most vivid figure of sin's isolating and excluding power, and the redemptive turn — Christ the great High Priest, who touched the leper and was not defiled but cleansed him, Matthew 8:3, and who himself 'suffered without the gate,' Hebrews 13:12, going to the place of the leper, bearing the reproach and exclusion, that the unclean might be brought near, 'let us go forth therefore unto him without the camp, bearing his reproach,' Hebrews 13:13, the figure of sin's separation healed by Christ who bore our exclusion); the leper defiled all the days of his plague (the continual uncleanness, the exclusion lasting as long as the plague) — the figure (the sinner defiled and excluded while the sin remains) — Calvin, Wesley, Keil, Gill.
